Import necessary libraries including PyTorch, torch.nn.functional, and torchvision.transforms for data augmentation.
Define the batch size as 32 and image dimensions as 224x224 for the ResNet model.
Create random input tensor with shape (batch_size, height, width, channels) using a uniform distribution between -1.0 and 1.0.
Define an augmentation sequence that includes RandomResizedCrop, RandomHorizontalFlip, and Normalize.
Apply the augmentation sequence to the random input tensor to generate augmented images for both student and teacher encoders.
Instantiate the ResNet model by calling the `student_encoder` method with hidden_layer = -1, projection_hidden_size = 256, num_classes_K = 1000, and projection_layers = 4.
Create a copy of the student encoder to serve as the teacher encoder without setting its gradients to None (i.e., keep it trainable).
Reset the moving average for the teacher encoder by deleting and recreating it.
Update the moving average once to initialize the teacher's weights.
Set student_temp = 0.9 and teacher_temp = 0.04 as specified in the Dino class initialization.
Call the forward method of the Dino instance with both augmented images from the first local crop, calculating the loss between the projections.
Verify that the calculated loss contains NaN values by checking if any element in the loss tensor is not finite (i.e., `torch.isnan(loss).any()` returns True).
Monitor the GPU memory usage during execution of the forward pass.
Assert that the GPU memory usage exceeds a predefined threshold, indicating that the bug has been reproduced.
